comment: E23 distribution assignment example (docs/mcp-bundle-distribution.md). Routes the team:core-ai4sale audience through the 'stable' channel of examples/mcp/bundle-channel.example.json in PIN mode: activate exactly the named bundle sha256 (the channel example's current pointer) and ignore channel movement until this assignment is reissued. In 'follow' mode the bundle_sha256 member MUST be absent (exactly one artifact owns the pointer -- the channel). The sha256 is a PLACEHOLDER (repeated single hex digit, clearly fake) and the signature value is base64 of 64 zero bytes, clearly fake -- E23 is design-only and nothing verifies it. The routed bundle still passes the unchanged E14 verification against the member's E15 trust store, including the bundle's OWN audience binding.

channel.comment: The FULL channel identity pair (name + owner key id): an assignment can never be satisfied by a same-named channel from a different owner.
channel.name: stable

issuer.comment: Trust decisions use key_id plus the member's local trusted-keys file only; display grants nothing.

signature.comment: PLACEHOLDER signature: base64 of 64 zero bytes -- obviously fake, for shape illustration only. Optional in the format (MIT core); REQUIRED for registered/business-tier distribution. A real value is the detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed.
